title: Automated Unit Tests for All Critical Deterministic Business Logic
description: The system shall maintain automated unit tests for all critical deterministic business logic. Deterministic logic includes eligibility date calculation, premium calculation, dependent age-out, enrollment validation, carrier simulation response, EDI 834 generation, and payroll deduction calculation.

testStrategy.architecture: E2E Tests → Integration Tests + AI Workflow Tests → Unit Tests → Business Rule Engine

title: Eligibility Date Calculation Unit Tests — Month-End, February, Leap Year, Year Boundary
description: The system shall maintain unit tests for eligibility date calculations covering all edge cases. Tests must resolve the previously identified ambiguity around 30-day waiting period vs. first-of-following-month activation.
mandatoryEdgeCases: Hire Date = last day of month

title: Premium Calculation Unit Tests — All Pay Frequencies
description: The system shall maintain unit tests for premium calculations across Weekly, Biweekly, Semi-monthly, and Monthly pay frequencies.
roundingSpecification: All payroll deductions shall be rounded to two decimal places using standard half-up rounding. Any cumulative rounding difference shall be reconciled in the final payroll period of the plan year.

title: Premium Rounding and Final-Period Reconciliation Tests
description: The system shall maintain unit tests for premium rounding method (half-up to 2 decimal places), decimal precision, floating-point error prevention, and final-period true-up reconciliation ensuring total annual deductions match annual premium exactly.
verifyItems: Final-period reconciliation: sum(all deductions) = annual premium

title: Dependent Age-Out Boundary Condition Tests
description: The system shall maintain unit tests for dependent age-out boundary conditions. All expected results shall be driven by the configured policy rule, not hardcoded age 26.
testBoundaries: Age 26 — coverage active through end of birth month
End of birthday month — last day of coverage
First day of following month — termination effective date

title: Life Event Enrollment Window Unit Tests — All Event Types and Boundaries
description: The system shall maintain unit tests for all configured life event enrollment windows and boundary conditions. Tests shall cover Event Date, Enrollment Date, Window Start, Window End, and Expected Result for each configured event type.

title: Business Rule Unit Tests — Eligibility, Enrollment, Carrier Simulation, Payroll, EDI 834
description: The system shall maintain unit tests for all configured business rules across eligibility, enrollment validation, carrier simulation deterministic responses, payroll deductions, and EDI 834 generation.
enrollmentTests: New Hire during waiting period → Rejected
deterministicNote: The same input must always generate the same result in DETERMINISTIC mode.

title: Integration Tests Covering Enrollment-to-Carrier, Enrollment-to-Payroll, EDI Generation, and Reconciliation
description: The system shall maintain integration tests validating complete service interactions across four flows.

integrationFlows.name: Reconciliation
integrationFlows.description: Verify configured benefitsPersonId → HR employeeId / Carrier memberId / Payroll employeeId mapping reconciles correctly

title: End-to-End Tests — Five Critical User Journeys
description: The system shall maintain end-to-end tests covering at minimum five critical Group Benefits user journeys.

title: Automated Negative Scenario and Validation Exception Tests
description: The system shall maintain automated tests for negative scenarios and validation exceptions including carrier rejection, dependent validation failure, enrollment outside window, field validation errors, and business rule violations.

title: Deterministic Seed Data for Repeatable Automated Testing
description: The system shall maintain a deterministic seed dataset for repeatable automated testing. The test dataset shall include Acme Corp with 30 employees spanning employment classes, 10 dependents, 9 benefit plans, 5 carriers, 20 enrollments, 5 life events, 5 eligibility exceptions, and 10 carrier transactions.

title: Golden Datasets for AI Requirement Extraction Evaluation
description: The system shall maintain golden datasets for evaluating AI requirement extraction quality. Each golden dataset pairs a source document with its expected extraction output.

goldenDatasetStructure.expected: Validated list of requirements expected to be extracted
goldenDatasetStructure.evaluation: Compare AI extraction output against expected set

title: AI-Generated Requirements Evaluation for Accuracy, Completeness, Traceability, and Hallucination
description: The system shall evaluate AI-generated requirements against golden datasets measuring: requirement extraction precision, recall, field extraction accuracy, conflict detection accuracy, impact analysis accuracy, hallucination rate, and citation/source traceability.
evaluationMetrics: Requirement extraction precision (correctly extracted / total extracted)
Requirement extraction recall (correctly extracted / total expected)
Field extraction accuracy (correct field values / total fields)
Hallucination rate (AI claims not grounded in source documents)
Citation traceability (AI output references source document and page)

title: Automated Tests Execute as Part of Application Build or CI/CD Process
description: Automated tests shall execute as part of the application build or CI/CD pipeline. CI/CD gate order: Commit → Lint → Unit Tests → Integration Tests → Build → E2E Smoke Tests → Deploy Demo.

title: Critical Test Failures Block Demo Build Promotion
description: Critical unit, integration, and E2E test failures shall prevent a successful demo build from being promoted. Minimum mandatory gate: Unit Tests MUST PASS, Integration Tests MUST PASS, Critical E2E Tests MUST PASS, Build MUST PASS.
